title: ODHS DISASTER CASE MANAGEMENT HOTLINE - 211info
text: OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES ODHS DISASTER CASE MANAGEMENT HOTLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF This program is designed to help survivors of specific natural disaster events connect to ODHS Disaster Case Managers. Intake does not guarantee assistance. Disaster case workers may be able to assist individuals in identifying and applying for disaster recovery support or addressing urgent needs. Services will vary based on eligibility and need. 500 Summer Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Residents of Oregon who have been affected by natural disasters Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (833) 669-0554 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (503) 945-5600 Main phone (503) 945-6214 TTY (503) 581-6198 FAX Hotline (833) 669-0554 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for intake. Information will be documented and submitted confidentially to ODHS and ODHS will follow up with the caller as soon as possible. Caller may leave a voicemail if specialists are unavailable. FEES: None for referrals DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: State of Oregon Services: Disaster Related Case Management Post Disaster Food Services Post Disaster Housing Assistance Other Services Offered At This Location: EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - YOUTH EXPERIENCING HOMELESSNESS PROGRAM INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - ODHS LANGUAGE ACCESS AND ACCOMMODATIONS INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - ODHS LOCAL OFFICES INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - SAFE ABUSE REPORTING HOTLINE LAW ENFORCEMENT SERVICES - ODHS FRAUD INVESTIGATIONS PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - OREGON ONE ELIGIBILITY PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - REFUGEE SERVICES SPECIALIZED TREATMENT AND PREVENTION - CHILDREN'S EXTRAORDINARY NEEDS PROGRAM If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES
